column3: Proto-Tree of Life, Mistress of Beasts
column4: Tree as axis mundi; goddess flanked by animals (lions, ibexes); "Mistress of Lions" motif

column4: Sacred tree with kings or gods as shepherds; animals flanking tree

column3: Asherah, Tree of Life, Pastoral Deities
column4: Tree or pole as goddess symbol; shepherd-king motifs; solar and fertility aspects
column5: Asherah, Baal, Apollo (solar/shepherd aspects), Mars (as agricultural/pastoral god)
column6: Asherah as consort of El/Yahweh, symbolized by trees/poles; Mars originally a pastoral/agricultural god before becoming a god of war145. Apollo as solar and pastoral deity in Greece.

column4: God with animals, vines, tree symbolism; ecstatic rites

column3: Tree of Life, Brazen Serpent, Solar and Messiah Figures
column4: Tree as symbol of life; serpent on staff (Moses); solar deities; messianic kings; shepherd imagery
column6: Moses' brazen serpent echoes serpent/tree motifs; Mithras as solar savior; Christ as "Good Shepherd" and tree/cross symbolism9.

column4: Mithras slaying bull; cosmic tree/cave; solar rays; caduceus (staff with serpents)
column6: Mithraic iconography includes cosmic tree/cave, solar rays, and savior motifs; Hermes' caduceus as healing/tree-serpent symbol.

column3: Chakra/Tree of Life Syncretism, Master of Beasts
column4: Chakras as subtle centers; tree as world axis; shamanic "master of beasts" motifs
column6: Chakra system as vertical axis; shamanic figures as mediators between worlds, often depicted with animals and world tree9.
